text.config.zulip-bridge.option.zulipBas:
Full URL of your Zulip organisation.
Example: https://myorg.zulipchat.com
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.botEmail:
Email address of the Zulip account used by this Minecraft client.
Each player can use their own account locally.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.botApiKe:
API key for the Zulip account used by this Minecraft client.
Generate one at Settings → Personal settings → Account & privacy.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.streamNa: Name of the Zulip stream (channel) to bridge.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.topicNam: Topic within the stream to bridge.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.messageT: Choose whether the bridge uses a stream/topic or a direct-message conversation.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.directMe:
Comma-separated Zulip API email addresses for the DM conversation.
Do not include your own email.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.commandN:
Local Minecraft command used to send to Zulip.
Example: 'zulip' means /zulip send hello.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.pollInte:
How often to check Zulip for new messages.
Lower values are more responsive but use more API calls.
Enable or disable the bridge. Changes apply immediately while in-game.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.suppress: Hide incoming Zulip messages authored by the configured account to avoid duplicates.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.playerDi: Name shown in Zulip when you chat. Leave blank to use your in-game name.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.prependP:
If enabled, outgoing Zulip messages are prefixed with your Minecraft player name.
If disabled, Zulip only shows your Zulip account name as the sender.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.incoming: Text prepended to Zulip messages shown in Minecraft chat.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.showInco:
Show or hide the Zulip prefix/badge on incoming messages.
Only the sender color will be shown when disabled.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.senderCo:
Hex color for incoming sender names.
Use #RRGGBB, for example #3A9E5C.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.messageC:
Hex color for incoming message text.
Use #RRGGBB, for example #50C878.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.incoming: Choose whether incoming Zulip messages are stripped to plain text or shown as raw Markdown.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.playInco: Play a local Minecraft UI sound when a Zulip message arrives.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.showInco: Show a client-side toast notification when a Zulip message arrives.
text.config.zulip-bridge.option.showSucc: Print a local chat line when a Zulip message is sent successfully. Failed sends are always shown.
